title: Maytenus bureaviana
taxonomy: Maytenus bureaviana is classified within the family Celastraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Maytenus, which consists of about 100 species of flowering plants. The Celastraceae family, also known as the staff vine family, comprises around 100 genera and 1,300 species. The classification of Maytenus bureaviana is further supported by its characteristics, such as its evergreen leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant's classification is an essential aspect of understanding its unique features and behaviors.
uses: Maytenus bureaviana has been studied for its potential medicinal uses, including the treatment of various health conditions. side_effects: Maytenus bureaviana is known to be toxic and can cause a range of side effects due to its chemical composition.
